children.text: immediately shall issue a travel advisory for that airport; and

children.heading: .—Suspending Assistance
children.label: b .—Suspending Assistance
children.text: The President shall suspend assistance provided under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.) or the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.) to a country in which is located an airport with respect to which becomes effective if the Secretary of State decides the country is a high terrorist threat country. The President may waive this subsection if the President decides, and reports to Congress, that the waiver is required because of national security interests or a humanitarian emergency.22 U.S.C. 215122 U.S.C. 2751section 44907(d)(1) of this title

children.heading: .—Actions No Longer Required
children.label: c .—Actions No Longer Required
children.text: An action required under this section is no longer required only if the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration has made a decision as provided under . The Administrator shall notify Congress when the action is no longer required to be taken.section 44907(d)(4) of this title
